---
title: "Banana"
bibliography: references.bib
author:
  - name: Francisco Queiroz
    affiliations: School of Design, University of Leeds
  - name: Maeve Murphy Quinlan
    affiliations: Research Computing, University of Leeds

keywords:
  - pedagogical frameworks
  - teaching material
  
---

<!-- ::: {custom-style="BoxBody"} -->
<!-- BANANA BOX BODY -->
<!-- ::: -->

## Introduction

You find yourself in a large room with no windows; the air is still, and nothing suggests the passing of time. The door closes behind you, and it won't open for another hour or two. As you stand in front of the room, you realise there is nothing between you and what seems to be a party of the most intimidating, unpredictable creatures.

Students.

> some quote is here


::: {.banana  }
## 0The anarchist important:

0Another characteristic of anarchist pedagogies, particularly in the arts (and programming is often 
:::

::: {.callout-important title="oh yeah"}
## 1The anarchist important:

1Another characteristic of anarchist pedagogies, particularly in the arts (and programming is often 
:::

::: {.callout-note }
## The anarchist note:

Another characteristic of anarchist pedagogies, particularly in the arts (and programming is often 
:::

::: {.callout-warning}
## The anarchist warning:

Another characteristic of anarchist pedagogies, particularly in the arts (and programming is often 
:::

::: {.callout-tip }
## The anarchist tip:

Another characteristic of anarchist pedagogies, particularly in the arts (and programming is often 
:::

::: {.callout-caution }
## The anarchist caution:

Another characteristic of anarchist pedagogies, particularly in the arts (and programming is often 
:::



## Acknowledgement

The authors would like to thank Samantha Ahern and Lucia Michielin for their revision and feedback.


::: {.content-visible }
## Referencems {.unnumbered}
:::
